Legal

Privacy Policy

InfyniPay B.V. · Last updated: June 2026 · GDPR compliant

Your data, in plain language: We collect only what we need to run the service. We do not sell or rent your personal data to third parties. As an EU-based company, we process all data in accordance with the GDPR. You have the right to access, correct, and delete your data.

1. Who We Are

InfyniPay B.V. is the data controller for personal data processed through this website and the PaymentHood platform. We are incorporated under Dutch law and registered in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

You can reach our data protection contact at support@infynipay.com.

2. Information We Collect

Merchant Information

When you register as a merchant or business user, we collect:

  • Business name, legal form, and registration details;
  • Contact details: name, email address, phone number;
  • Account login credentials (passwords are hashed and never stored in plain text);
  • Billing and subscription information;
  • Transaction records and gateway configuration data.

Client / End-Customer Information

When end-customers complete a payment checkout via a merchant's integration, we may process:

  • Name, email address, billing and shipping address;
  • Phone number (where provided);
  • Tokenised payment method identifiers — we never store raw card numbers or full card credentials;
  • Transaction details including amount, currency, and timestamp.

Automatically Collected Information

When you interact with our website or platform, we automatically collect:

  • Log data: IP addresses, device identifiers, browser type, operating system;
  • API interaction and usage data;
  • Cookies and similar technologies used for authentication and analytics (see Section 7).

3. How We Use Your Information

We use personal data for the following purposes, each supported by a lawful basis under the GDPR:

  • Service provision (contract performance) — to operate the PaymentHood platform and process transactions on your behalf;
  • Billing and invoicing (contract performance) — to manage your subscription and payments to InfyniPay;
  • Legal compliance (legal obligation) — to meet our obligations under Dutch law, EU financial regulations, PSD2, and anti-money-laundering requirements;
  • Fraud prevention and security (legitimate interests) — to detect, investigate, and prevent fraudulent activity;
  • Service improvement (legitimate interests) — to analyse usage patterns and improve platform performance;
  • Communications (legitimate interests / consent) — to send service updates, security alerts, and, where you have opted in, marketing communications.

4. Data Retention

We retain personal data only as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes outlined in this policy:

  • Active merchant account data is retained for the duration of the service relationship;
  • Transaction data is retained for a minimum of seven (7) years to comply with Dutch tax and financial record-keeping laws;
  • Marketing consent data is retained until you withdraw consent;
  • Log and technical data is typically retained for up to 90 days.

5. Data Sharing

We do not sell or rent personal data to third parties.

We may share data with the following categories of recipients, only to the extent necessary:

  • Payment processors and acquiring banks — to facilitate transaction routing and settlement;
  • Regulatory and law enforcement authorities — where required by law, court order, or to prevent fraud;
  • Infrastructure and cloud service providers — who process data on our behalf under data processing agreements;
  • Professional advisors — such as lawyers and accountants, bound by confidentiality obligations;
  • Successors in business — in the event of a merger, acquisition, or business transfer, subject to equivalent data protection obligations.

6. International Data Transfers

Where personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area (EEA), we ensure adequate protections are in place through one of the following mechanisms: EU Standard Contractual Clauses, adequacy decisions issued by the European Commission, or other approved transfer mechanisms under Chapter V of the GDPR.

7. Cookies

We use cookies and similar tracking technologies for:

  • Essential cookies — required for the platform to function (login sessions, security tokens);
  • Analytics cookies — to understand how users interact with our platform (aggregated, non-identifiable).

You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may affect platform functionality.

8. Security

We implement industry-standard technical and organisational security measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure. These include encryption in transit (TLS), encryption at rest, access controls, and regular security assessments.

No method of transmission over the internet is completely secure. While we take all reasonable precautions, we cannot guarantee absolute security.

9. Your Rights Under GDPR

If you are located in the EEA, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

  • Right of access — to obtain a copy of the personal data we hold about you;
  • Right to rectification — to request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data;
  • Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten") — to request deletion of your data where there is no compelling reason to continue processing;
  • Right to restriction — to request that we limit processing of your data in certain circumstances;
  • Right to data portability — to receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format;
  • Right to object — to object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing;
  • Right to withdraw consent — where processing is based on consent, to withdraw it at any time without affecting prior processing.

Merchant users may exercise these rights directly via their account or by contacting support@infynipay.com. End-customers should direct requests to the merchant from whom they purchased.

If you believe your rights have not been respected, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) at autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl.

10. Merchant Obligations

Merchants using InfyniPay or PaymentHood are independent data controllers for the personal data of their own customers. Merchants are responsible for maintaining a lawful basis for processing end-customer data, publishing their own privacy notices, and complying with all applicable data protection laws in their jurisdiction.

11. Children's Privacy

Our services are not directed at individuals under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors. If you believe we have inadvertently collected such data, please contact us immediately.

12. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or applicable law. We will notify merchants of material changes via email or a prominent notice on the platform. The date at the top of this page indicates when the policy was last revised.

13. Contact

For any questions, requests, or concerns relating to this Privacy Policy or your personal data, please contact us:

InfyniPay B.V.
Amsterdam, Netherlands
Email: support@infynipay.com

Payment infrastructure built for European businesses. Powering the future of digital commerce.
